texts.orig: 1. Introduction
texts.text: 1. Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Membranes have become a promising technology in the area of gas separation due to their compactness, energy savings, reduced capital investment cost, simple operation, easy o ff shore installation, and the convenient integration with any other separation system. For designing the membrane, mathematical modeling and simulation are very useful tools as they can reduce the number of experiments, the time, and the costs of the designing process. In addition to the system ' s simulation, optimization techniques can be used to fi nd more e ffi cient designs for the membranes.
texts.text: Membranes have become a promising technology in the area of gas separation due to their compactness, energy savings, reduced capital investment cost, simple operation, easy o ff shore installation, and the convenient integration with any other separation system. For designing the membrane, mathematical modeling and simulation are very useful tools as they can reduce the number of experiments, the time, and the costs of the designing process. In addition to the system ' s simulation, optimization techniques can be used to fi nd more e ffi cient designs for the membranes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A number of papers have been published in the open literature focusing on the optimization of membrane-based gas separation systems. Several researchers performed the optimization studies considering the membrane con fi guration as one of the optimization targets. Qi and Henson (2000) performed a simultaneous optimization of a permeator con fi guration and its operating conditions using the mixed integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) design technique [1]. Lababidi et al. developed mathematical models to optimize three con fi gurations for membrane gas separation modules including a single stage, two stages, and the continuous membrane column [2]. Datta and Sen focused on fi nding the optimum con fi guration and design variables for the asymmetric membrane-based separation of carbon dioxide from natural gas for satisfying the pipeline speci fi cation of 2% of carbon dioxide [3]. texts.orig: A B S T R A C T
texts.text: A B S T R A C T

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A novel optimization method, based on a mathematical modeling approach, for the design and operation of a hollow fi ber membrane module is developed. The purpose of the membrane is to purify unconventional gases, such as coalbed methane gas, by removing carbon dioxide. For the design of the membrane module, the number of fi bers within the module must be reasonably set as one of the design variables of the optimization problem. Therefore, a mixed integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) model including integer variables -i.e., the number of fi bers in this study -is required to obtain optimal design conditions. However, if the integer variable is involved in the highly nonlinear model, the probability of success in the optimization calculation becomes very low because of the big computation load. Thus, this paper introduces: (1) a new optimization modeling technique which is able to solve the MINLP problem e ffi ciently; (2) a successful scale up design optimization from laboratory (0.5 Nm 3 /h) to large commercial scale (500 Nm 3 /h); (3) design optimization results under di ff erent gas feed conditions, and (4) dynamic optimization results obtained by varying gas feed conditions.
texts.text: A novel optimization method, based on a mathematical modeling approach, for the design and operation of a hollow fi ber membrane module is developed. The purpose of the membrane is to purify unconventional gases, such as coalbed methane gas, by removing carbon dioxide. For the design of the membrane module, the number of fi bers within the module must be reasonably set as one of the design variables of the optimization problem. Therefore, a mixed integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) model including integer variables -i.e., the number of fi bers in this study -is required to obtain optimal design conditions. However, if the integer variable is involved in the highly nonlinear model, the probability of success in the optimization calculation becomes very low because of the big computation load. Thus, this paper introduces: (1) a new optimization modeling technique which is able to solve the MINLP problem e ffi ciently; (2) a successful scale up design optimization from laboratory (0.5 Nm 3 /h) to large commercial scale (500 Nm 3 /h); (3) design optimization results under di ff erent gas feed conditions, and (4) dynamic optimization results obtained by varying gas feed conditions.

Purnomo and Alpay designed and analyzed two generalized membrane con fi gurations with recycle streams for the bulk air separation [5] by using the equation oriented gPROMS modeling software [6] with successive reduced quadratic programming (SRQP) optimization solver. Genetic algorithms were also employed for the membrane optimization by Marriot and Sørensen [7], and Chang and Hou [8]. Marriot and Sørensen reported an optimal design strategy for membrane separation systems based on genetic algorithms [7]. Chang and Hou applied genetic algorithm for the optimization of the membrane gas separation system with single and triple objective functions [8]. Tessendorf et al. introduced a mathematical model and a numerical solution procedure for the separation of multicomponents mixtures using a membrane gas module and implemented the simulation modules in the industrial equation oriented simulation and optimization environment, called OPTISIM developed by Linde AG [9].

texts.orig: Though a number of optimization studies have been carried out, there have been few works focusing on the optimization of the membrane module design based on a dynamic simulation model. The dynamic model can predict the dynamic behavior of the systems according to variations on the system ' s conditions within time and unexpected disturbances in real site situations. Thus, this paper focuses on the design optimization of the membrane module and the dynamic optimization of the system ' s operation. The adopted system is a hollow fi ber membrane module with counter-current fl ow for CO2 removal

texts.orig: As indicated in Tables 3.1 and 3.2, the new approach using only NLP solver shows a much better performance when compared to the conventional method using MINLP solver. The main reason is because the NLP model does not need to treat the integer variable, as the optimal integer variable (the number of fi bers in the module) is automatically obtained using the new algorithm, which results in a smaller computation load for the optimization. The current optimization results from the MINLP solver (OAERAP) show that the optimal values of integer variables tend to be close to the bound when the feed fl ows are 0.5 Nm 3 /h and 10 Nm 3 /h and the number of MINLP iterations is just two. These phenomena may result from the fact that the MINLP solver may try to fi nd the optimal values by adjusting more continuous variables than discrete variable (the number of fi bers) in the present cases, because the MINLP algorithm (OAERAP) follows the steps described below [15 -18]:

However, the search for the optimal integer variable value may not be necessary when the optimization tolerance is already satis fi ed by fi nding the optimal values of continuous variables. On the other hand, if the new approach is adopted, the NLP solver can obtain the optimal values of both discrete and continuous variables properly and e ffi ciently. The total CPU times for the optimizations of the NLP problems range from 35 s to 582 s for the feed fl ow scale ranging from 0.5 Nm 3 /h to 500 Nm 3 /h. However, the optimization computation times of MINLP problems take from a few hundred seconds to a few hundred thousand seconds when the feed fl ow scale ranges from 0.5 Nm 3 /h to 10 Nm 3 /h. When the feed fl ow scale was 50 Nm 3 /h, the optimization of the MINLP model was running for more than a month and aborted because it seems to be not converging. The trials of the MINLP optimizations for the scale up design from 50 Nm 3 /h to 500 Nm 3 /h fail to converge.

texts.orig: Fig. 5 and Fig. 6 illustrate the trends of the optimal conditions when the feed pressure is also treated as a decision variable according to the various fl ow rates and the various feed compositions respectively. Under highest feed fl ow rate condition, the optimal values of decision variables such as feed pressure, number of fi bers within a module, effective length, and shell inside diameter, become bigger and the optimal stage cuts show the same constant value of 0.265 as the base case as shown in Fig. 5. Fig. 6 describes an interesting behavior: the number of fi bers and the e ff ective area do not monotonically increase as the methane feed mole fraction decreases. This result is because the high feed pressure and stage cut can overcome the in fl uence coming from the reduced e ff ective area. In other words, in this case, the feed pressure and the stage cut have stronger in fl uences on the methane purity and recovery than the e ff ective area. The optimal shell inside diameter does not monotonically increase either as the methane feed mole fraction decreases. The shell diameter has a lower in fl uence on the performance than the feed pressure and stage cut.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4. Conclusion
texts.text: 4. Conclusion

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This study proposes a new optimization modeling approach which can easily solve the conventional MINLP problem of the hollow fi ber membrane model. The suggested optimization model converts the integer variable into a continuous variable in the optimization model, instead of solving the MINLP problem directly, which is di ffi cult to converge in the optimization computations. Therefore, the new algorithm can e ffi ciently solve the NLP problem as it requires the same model information as the conventional MINLP problem and delivers quickly the optimal values of the integer variable as well as those of the continuous variables.
texts.text: This study proposes a new optimization modeling approach which can easily solve the conventional MINLP problem of the hollow fi ber membrane model. The suggested optimization model converts the integer variable into a continuous variable in the optimization model, instead of solving the MINLP problem directly, which is di ffi cult to converge in the optimization computations. Therefore, the new algorithm can e ffi ciently solve the NLP problem as it requires the same model information as the conventional MINLP problem and delivers quickly the optimal values of the integer variable as well as those of the continuous variables.

texts.text: Analyzing the results of the optimization case studies, the following facts are perceived:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1) As the feed fl ow rate increases, the optimal values for the e ff ective area or the feed pressure must increase to satisfy the methane purity and recovery constraints.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2) With the decrease of methane feed mole fraction, the optimal effective length and stage cut need to increase to meet the requirements of methane purity and recovery.
texts.text: With the decrease of methane feed mole fraction, the optimal effective length and stage cut need to increase to meet the requirements of methane purity and recovery.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3) The e ff ect of the shell inside diameter on the performance seems to be very small compared to that of the e ff ective area, the stage cut, and the feed pressure.
